title: cerinthe major subsp. purpurascens
intro: Cerinthe major subsp. purpurascens, also known as the Purple Honey Flower, is a subspecies of the annual flowering plant Cerinthe major, belonging to the borage family (Boraginaceae). This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, making it useful for treating various health issues such as digestive problems and skin conditions. The health benefits of Cerinthe major subsp. purpurascens include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and promote wound healing, among others. The plant is typically prepared for herbal use through infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and provide relief from various health conditions.
